keyRules: Maximum flight altitude 120 m above ground level (EASA Open category)
Register as a UAS operator (ISK 5,863 / 5 years) if your drone weighs 250 g or more or has a camera
The A1/A3 online course is mandatory — minimum age 16
Restrictions in volcanic eruption areas: always check current eruption activity
National parks (Vatnajökull, Þingvellir, Snæfellsjökull): from 17 May 2026 recreational drone flying is refused in 25+ protected areas (decision under appeal)
Always keep the drone within visual line of sight (VLOS)
Never fly over people, crowds or public events
At least 2 km from commercial airports, 1.5 km from smaller airfields and landing sites
Difficult wind conditions: in Iceland the wind often exceeds drone safety limits
importantNotes: Active volcanic eruption areas may have temporary flight bans — check before every flight

From 17 May 2026 the Nature Conservation Agency refuses recreational and instructional drone flying in 25+ protected areas (national parks and more); the decision has been appealed to the Ministry of the Environment
Icelandic weather is volatile — the wind often exceeds drone limits
Bird nesting season (May–August): additional restrictions in many areas
Iceland is in the EEA but not the EU — EASA rules apply through the EEA Agreement

fields.disclaimerText: Always check the official sources of Samgöngustofa (ICETRA) and FlyDrone.is before flying. This app is a tool — not a legal authority. Iceland follows EASA rules through the EEA Agreement with national additions.
